chapter_title.en: Chapter 87 Abhorrence of the extravagance and the miserliness

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/186)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/186)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ الْأَوَّلَ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ النَّفَقَةِ عَلَى الْعِيَالِ فَقَالَ مَا بَيْنَ الْمَكْرُوهَيْنِ الْإِسْرَافِ وَ الْإِقْتَارِ .

en.hubeali: And from him, from his father, from Muhammad Bin Amro, from Abdullah Bin Aban who said,

‘I asked Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> the 1<sup>st</sup>, about the spending upon the dependants. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘What is between the two (limits of) abhorrence – the extravagance and the miserliness’.
en.sarwar: 2. It is narrated from him (narrator of previous Hadith) from his father from Muhammad ibn ‘Ammar from ‘Abd Allah ibn Aban who has said the following: “Once I asked abu al-Hassan, the 1st, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the expenses of one’s dependents. He (the Imam) replied, ‘It is between the two detestable limits: namely, excessive spending and severe austerity.’”
